description: Generate zones based on an algorithm—generally from some other data structure. The following keywords are available:

6.0.description: This set of commands generates zones based on an algorithm, generally from some other data structure. The following keywords are available:

keywords.name: from-building-blocks
keywords.description: Generate zones from the current building-blocks set. See the Building-Blocks section. If the optional keyword set is used, then will generate zones from the set with name s instead of the current set.

keywords.name: from-extruder
keywords.description: Generate zones from the current extruder set.

keywords.name: from-extruder-area-check
keywords.description: Specify if areas of underlying sub-elements should be checked when generating zones. Default value is true. An error occurs if Area-Check is true and the area of a triangular sub-element in each overlay \(\le\) 10% of the whole element area (in 2D Extrusion Pane).

keywords.name: from-topography
keywords.description: Generates zones by extruding surface faces in the range until they meet polygons in a geometric set.

keywords.name: from-topography-geometry-set
keywords.description: Specify the geometry set used to define the surface to which zones are being extruded. By default the current geometric set will be used.

keywords.name: from-topography-segments
keywords.description: Specify the number of zone layers created between the existing surface zone faces and the geometry set. By default this value is 1.

keywords.name: from-topography-direction
keywords.description: Specify the extrusion direction that will be used to create zones on surface faces. From a surface gridpoint, a ray will be cast in this direction until it intersects a polygon in the geomtric set. This will define the shape and size of the zones created. By default this value will be (0,0,1). If a face normal is parallel to this direction vector (with a tolerance of about 0.5 degrees) the face will be neglected even it if is in the range.

keywords.name: from-topography-ratio
keywords.description: Specify the ratio used to distribute zones from the surface face to the geometric set. A ratio of 1.1 means that each zone edge in that direction will be 1.1 times the length of the previous zone. If not specified, the default ratio is 1.0.

keywords.name: from-topography-group
keywords.description: Assign the group s1 to the created zones, in slot s2. If not specified, the default slot is Default. Use of the group logic is described in Group.

keywords.name: from-topography-face-group
keywords.description: Assign the group s1 in slot s2 to the new surface faces created by extruding the original faces selected. By default no group name is assigned. If not specified, the slot used will be Default.

keywords.name: from-geometry
keywords.description: Generate zones by identifying closed volumes in a geometric set, and creating tetrahedral zones that fill the volume. The following optional keywords are available.

keywords.name: from-geometry-set
keywords.description: Specify the geometry set used to define the surface to which zones are being extruded. By default the current geometric set will be used.

keywords.name: from-geometry-maximum-edge
keywords.description: Specify the maximum edge length for any tetra to be generated. The actual maximum edge length will be close to this, but the result is not guaranteed to be exact.

keywords.name: from-geometry-gradation
keywords.description: Rate at which, on the surface of the tetrahedral mesh, neighboring triangle sizes vary as triangles increase or decrease in size due to local size adaptation.

keywords.name: from-geometry-offset
keywords.description: Offset distance between the input surface and the surface of the resulting tetrahedral mesh measured in thousandths of the length of the bounding box diagonal.

keywords.name: from-geometry-cut-angle
keywords.description: Threshold angle for the capture of surface features. The default value is 45°. Use a larger cut angle smooth over unwanted surface features associated with the coarseness of the triangulation. Use a smaller cut angle if if there are intersecting surfaces after surface remeshing.

keywords.name: from-geometry-verbose
keywords.description: The default value is off. If set to on the tetra generation process emits information and diagnostic information to the console.

keywords.name: from-geometry-hexahedron
keywords.description: If specified, then an attempt will be made to fill the volume with hexahedral blocks.

keywords.name: from-geometry-bring-to-building-blocks
keywords.description: If specified, then the result of the volume discretization will also be sent to a building-blocks set.

keywords.name: from-geometry-grid-file
keywords.description: If specified, then the result of the volume discretization will be sent to output file s (no zones generated). Grid-File keyword has no effect if Bring-To-Building-Blocks is present.

7.0.description: This set of commands generates zones based on an algorithm, generally from some other data structure. The following keywords are available:

keywords.name: from-building-blocks
keywords.description: Generate zones from the current building-blocks set. See the building-blocks command. If the optional keyword set is used, then will generate zones from the set with name s instead of the current set.

keywords.name: from-extruder
keywords.description: Generate zones from the current extruder set.

keywords.name: from-extruder-area-check
keywords.description: Specify if areas of underlying sub-elements should be checked when generating zones. Default values is ‘true’. An error will be generated if ‘Area-Check’ is set to ‘true’ and the area of a triangular sub-element in each overlay \(\le\) 10% of the whole element area (in 2D Extrusion Pane).

keywords.name: from-topography
keywords.description: Generates zones by extruding surface faces in the range until they meet polygons in a geometric set.

keywords.name: from-topography-geometry-set
keywords.description: Specify the geometry set used to define the surface to which zones are being extruded. By default the current geometric set will be used.

keywords.name: from-topography-segments
keywords.description: Specify the number of zone layers created between the existing surface zone faces and the geometry set. By default this value is 1.

keywords.name: from-topography-direction
keywords.description: Specify the extrusion direction that will be used to create zones on surface faces. From a surface gridpoint, a ray will be cast in this direction until it intersects a polygon in the geomtric set. This will define the shape and size of the zones created. By default this value will be (0,0,1). If a face normal is parallel to this direction vector (with a tolerance of about 0.5 degrees) the face will be neglected even it if is in the range.

keywords.name: from-topography-ratio
keywords.description: Specify the ratio used to distribute zones from the surface face to the geometric set. A ratio of 1.1 means that each zone edge in that direction will be 1.1 times the length of the previous zone. If not specified, the default ratio is 1.0.

keywords.name: from-topography-group
keywords.description: Assign the group s1 to the created zones, in slot s2. If not specified, the default slot is Default. Use of the group logic is described in Groups.

keywords.name: from-topography-face-group
keywords.description: Assign the group s1 in slot s2 to the new surface faces created by extruding the original faces selected. By default no group name is assigned. If not specified, the slot used will be Default.

keywords.name: from-geometry
keywords.description: Generate zones by identifying closed volumes in a geometric set, and creating zones that fill the volume. The comand operates in three steps: first, original surfaces (faces) are triangulated to create initial coarse mesh; second, the initial mesh is remeshed with triangular or mixed (quad-dominant mode) elements using user specified parameters; lastly, volume mesher is called to fill the volumes with tetrahedral or mixed (hex-dominant mode) zones. By deaults, hex-dominant mode is used (quad-dominant for surface meshes). Note that input surfaces should be planar for proper triangulation.

keywords.name: from-geometry-set
keywords.description: Specify the geometry set used to define the surface to which zones are being extruded. By default the current geometric set will be used.

keywords.name: from-geometry-minimum-edge
keywords.description: Specify the minimum surface element size f > 0 along the edges. The actual size will be close to this value, but the result is not guaranteed to be exact.

keywords.name: from-geometry-maximum-edge
keywords.description: Specify the maximum surface element size f > 0 along the edges. The actual size will be close to this value, but the result is not guaranteed to be exact.

keywords.name: from-geometry-size-surface
keywords.description: Specify desired elements size on surface meshes. The elements size tends (according to the Gradation-Surface parameter) toward this value (if > 0) as they get away from the edges. This parameter can be used to make surface elements finer or coarser. The default value of 0 means that the size will be determined automaticaly based on the element sizes along the edges.

keywords.name: from-geometry-gradation-surface
keywords.description: Specify gradation f > 0 of the element sizes along the edges (gradation from Minimum-Edge to Maximum-Edge) and inside the surface meshes (when Size-Surface is specified). A value close to 0 leads to a more progressive variation. The default value is 0.5.

keywords.name: from-geometry-size-volume
keywords.description: Specify desired zone size inside the closed volumes. The zones size tends toward this value as they get away from the bounding surfaces (surface meshes). The default value of 0 means that the size will be determined automaticaly based on the size of surface elements.

keywords.name: from-geometry-gradation-volume
keywords.description: Specify gradation f > 0 of the zone sizes from the surface elements sizes to the size defined by Size-Volume parameter inside the closed volumes. A value close to 0 leads to a more progressive variation. The default value is 0.5.

keywords.name: from-geometry-cut-angle
keywords.description: Specify cut angle for delimiting initial surfaces patches (groups of connected triangles in the initial triangulated mesh) used for surface remeshing. An angle between two adjacent triangles greater than this value will draw a limit between two patches. A small value tends to give numerous small patches and the final mesh will be close to the initial mesh but may be of poor quality. A bigger value tends to give fewer bigger patches which are better for remeshing but the final mesh may be more distant from the initial mesh (increased geometric error). The default value is 20° and a value of 45° should be considered as the maximum.

keywords.name: from-geometry-tetrahedron
keywords.description: If specified, only tetrahedral zones will be generated.

keywords.name: from-geometry-bring-to-building-blocks
keywords.description: If specified, then the result of the volume discretization will also be sent to a building-blocks set.

keywords.name: from-geometry-grid-file
keywords.description: If specified, then the result of the volume discretization will be sent to output file s (no zones generated). Grid-File keyword has no effect if Bring-To-Building-Blocks is present.

keywords.name: from-building-blocks
keywords.description: Generate zones from the current building-blocks model. Optional set s selects a named building-blocks set. (3D only.)

keywords.name: from-geometry
keywords.description: Generate zones by tetrahedral meshing of a closed geometry set. Sub-keywords control the meshing: set, minimum-edge, maximum-edge, size-surface, gradation-surface, size-volume, gradation-volume, cut-angle, tetrahedron, bring-to-building-blocks, grid-file.

keywords.name: from-geometry-set
keywords.description: Name of the geometry set to mesh.

keywords.name: from-geometry-minimum-edge
keywords.description: Minimum allowed edge length in the generated mesh.

keywords.name: from-geometry-maximum-edge
keywords.description: Maximum allowed edge length.

keywords.name: from-geometry-size-surface
keywords.description: Target element size on the closed surface.

keywords.name: from-geometry-gradation-surface
keywords.description: Surface gradation factor (controls element size growth along the surface).

keywords.name: from-geometry-size-volume
keywords.description: Target element size in the interior volume.

keywords.name: from-geometry-gradation-volume
keywords.description: Volume gradation factor (controls element size growth into the interior).

keywords.name: from-geometry-cut-angle
keywords.description: Feature-edge detection angle, in degrees.

keywords.name: from-geometry-tetrahedron
keywords.description: Force pure tetrahedral output (no zone merging into pentahedra/hexahedra).

keywords.name: from-geometry-bring-to-building-blocks
keywords.description: Also create matching building blocks from the generated geometry.

keywords.name: from-geometry-grid-file
keywords.description: Write the generated mesh to the named grid-file in addition to creating zones.

keywords.name: from-sketch
keywords.description: Generate zones from the current sketch (the 9.0 replacement for 7.0's from-extruder workflow). Sub-keyword: area-check.

keywords.name: from-sketch-area-check
keywords.description: Enable/disable per-block area-consistency checks during sketch-driven zone generation.

keywords.name: from-topography
keywords.description: Generate zones by extruding from a topographic surface. Sub-keywords: geometry-set, segments, direction, ratio, group, face-group.

keywords.name: from-topography-geometry-set
keywords.description: Name of the geometry set holding the topographic surface.

keywords.name: from-topography-segments
keywords.description: Number of zone layers in the extrusion direction.

keywords.name: from-topography-direction
keywords.description: Extrusion direction vector.

keywords.name: from-topography-ratio
keywords.description: Geometric ratio between consecutive layer thicknesses (for graded layering).

keywords.name: from-topography-group
keywords.description: Assign the extruded zones to group s1 (in optional slot s2).

keywords.name: from-topography-face-group
keywords.description: Assign the bottom face of the extrusion to face-group s1 (in optional slot s2).
